# UC-17.3.43 · FortiSASE Admin Configuration Audit (Fortinet)

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Configuration, Compliance

*Every time an administrator changes our FortiSASE security settings, we record it. We especially watch for deletions of security rules and changes made at unusual hours, because a single wrong change to our cloud security system can affect every employee at once.*

---

## Description

FortiSASE policies are centrally managed and affect all connected users globally. Configuration audit logs enable change tracking, compliance, and root-cause analysis when policies cause access issues.

## Value

Security governance teams audit all FortiSASE administrative configuration changes, flagging security profile deletions, out-of-hours modifications, and high-impact changes to firewall policies, ZTNA rules, and web filter profiles that could create immediate security gaps.

## Implementation

Forward FortiSASE management audit events. Alert on after-hours changes or bulk policy modifications. Correlate with ITSM change tickets.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- FortiSASE admin audit events in `index=fortisase` with `sourcetype=fortisase:event` or `sourcetype=fortisase:audit`. Key fields: `user` (admin), `action` (login/logout/add/edit/delete), `msg` (description), `cfgpath` (configuration path changed), `cfgobj` (object name), `devname`.
- FortiSASE configuration changes can have immediate impact: modifying a security profile, changing ZTNA rules, or altering SD-WAN policies affect all connected users and sites.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify admin audit events:
```spl
index=fortisase sourcetype IN ("fortisase:event","fortisase:audit") earliest=-30d
| where isnotnull(cfgpath) OR match(action, "(?i)add|edit|delete")
| stats count by action, cfgpath
```

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Configuration change audit:
```spl
index=fortisase sourcetype IN ("fortisase:event","fortisase:audit") earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(cfgpath) OR match(action, "(?i)add|edit|delete")
| eval change_hour=strftime(_time, "%H")
| eval out_of_hours=if(change_hour<"08" OR change_hour>"18", "YES", "no")
| table _time, user, action, cfgpath, cfgobj, msg, out_of_hours
| sort - _time
```

#### Understanding this SPL: Lists all FortiSASE configuration changes with timing. Changes to `firewall/policy`, `webfilter/profile`, `ips/sensor`, or `ztna/rule` are high-impact. Out-of-hours changes are flagged. A deleted security profile may leave policies without protection.

High-impact changes:
```spl
index=fortisase sourcetype IN ("fortisase:event","fortisase:audit") earliest=-7d
| where match(cfgpath, "(?i)policy|profile|sensor|ztna|security") OR action="delete"
| stats count by user, action, cfgpath
| sort - count
```

### Step 3 — Validate
(a) Make a test config change. Verify audit event.
(b) In FortiSASE: Log & Report > Event Log > System Events.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Changes per day. Row 2 — High-impact changes. Row 3 — Out-of-hours.
Alert: Immediate on security profile deletion.

Runbook:
1. **Profile deleted** — May break dependent policies. Verify and restore.
2. **Out-of-hours change** — Verify with admin. Document justification.
3. **Many changes by one admin** — Could be automation or a bulk mistake.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **No audit events** — Event logging may be filtered. Check FortiSASE log settings.
- **`cfgpath` empty** — Login/logout events don't include config paths. Filter for config changes only.

## SPL

```spl
index=sase sourcetype="fgt_event" subtype="system" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(msg),"(?i)config|policy|rule|admin.*login|object.*modified")
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(msg) as actions by user, ui
| sort -last
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (admin changes), Timeline (configuration events), Bar chart (changes by admin).

## Known False Positives

Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.

## References

- [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)
